input.prompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.content:

data.reasoningText: The user has an MSTest project with a `.testsettings` file and wants to migrate it to `.runsettings` after upgrading MSTest packages. Let me look at their files first, and also invoke the relevant skill for MSTest migration guidance.

data.content:
Your `.testsettings` file is ignored because modern MSTest (v3+) only supports `.runsettings`. Here's the migration:

**Key mappings from your file:**
| `.testsettings` | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `TestTimeout` property (30 000 ms) | `<MSTest><TestTimeout>` |
| Assembly resolution / deployment | `<MSTest><DeploymentEnabled>` |
| Code coverage data collector | `<DataCollectors>` section |

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <RunConfiguration>
    <!-- Global test timeout (ms) — replaces <Property name="TestTimeout"> -->
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>

  <MSTest>
    <!-- Replaces AssemblyResolution / DeploymentEnabled from .testsettings -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>

    <!-- Per-test timeout (ms) — replaces <Property name="TestTimeout"> -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
  </MSTest>

  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <!-- Replaces the CodeCoverage DataCollector from .testsettings -->
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.SingleFileCollector, Microsoft.CodeCoverage, Culture=neutral">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.reasoningText:
The user has:
1. A `.testsettings` file with test timeout (30000ms), assembly resolution, and a code coverage data collector
2. An MSTest 3.8.0 project targeting net8.0

`.testsettings` files are deprecated and ignored by modern MSTest/VSTest. They need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file. Let me create the equivalent `.runsettings` file and update the `.csproj` to reference it.

The key mappings:
- `TestTimeout` property → `<MSTest><TestTimeout>` in `.runsettings` or `[Timeout]` attribute


- Code coverage data collector → `<DataCollectors>` section in `.runsettings`
- Assembly resolution / deployment → `<MSTest><DeploymentEnabled>` in `.runsettings`

Now I'll build out the `.runsettings` file with these settings.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <RunConfiguration>
    <!-- Global test timeout (ms) — replaces <Property name="TestTimeout"> -->
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>

  <MSTest>
    <!-- Replaces AssemblyResolution / DeploymentEnabled from .testsettings -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>

    <!-- Per-test timeout (ms) — replaces <Property name="TestTimeout"> -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
  </MSTest>

  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <!-- Replaces the CodeCoverage DataCollector from .testsettings -->
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.SingleFileCollector, Microsoft.CodeCoverage, Culture=neutral">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content: Now wire it up in the `.csproj`:

Here's a summary of what changed:

1. **Created `local.runsettings`** — the modern replacement for `.testsettings`:
   - **Timeout**: `<TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>` under `<MSTest>` (per-test) and `<TestSessionTimeout>` (whole run).
   - **Deployment**: `<DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>` replaces the assembly resolution block.
   - **Code coverage**: Updated data collector URI to the v2.0 format under `<DataCollectors>`.

2. **Updated `TestProject.csproj`** — added `<RunSettingsFilePath>` so `dotnet test` picks up the new file automatically.

You can now **delete `local.testsettings`** — it is no longer read by the test runner. You can also pass the settings explicitly with:

```
dotnet test --settings local.runsettings
```
